variant_label: Oil production & trade — Iran average oil export level, last year (1985)
notes: Assumed baseline: Tehran maintained last year's (1985) average oil export level of about 1.6 million b/d. [cite: CIA Directorate of Intelligence, NESA M 86-20082, "Iran-Iraq: The Economic Balance", 6 June 1986, PDF p.3]

variant_label: Oil production & trade — Assumed oil price for 1986 FX-earnings estimate
notes: Estimate assumes an oil price of $13 per barrel. [cite: CIA Directorate of Intelligence, NESA M 86-20082, "Iran-Iraq: The Economic Balance", 6 June 1986, PDF p.3]

variant_label: Foreign exchange — Iran estimated drop in real foreign exchange earnings
notes: Assuming a $13/barrel price and no significant dollar appreciation, Iran faces a 50-55 percent drop in real foreign exchange earnings in 1986. [cite: CIA Directorate of Intelligence, NESA M 86-20082, "Iran-Iraq: The Economic Balance", 6 June 1986, PDF p.3]

variant_label: Oil production & trade — Iran intended oil export increase target
notes: Iran intends to increase oil exports to between 2 and 2.8 million b/d, though shortages of skilled personnel and facility deterioration will make this hard to sustain. [cite: CIA Directorate of Intelligence, NESA M 86-20082, "Iran-Iraq: The Economic Balance", 6 June 1986, PDF p.3]

variant_label: Oil production & trade — Iran oil exports, first four months of 1986 average
notes: Exports for the first four months of 1986 averaged 1.5 million b/d. [cite: CIA Directorate of Intelligence, NESA M 86-20082, "Iran-Iraq: The Economic Balance", 6 June 1986, PDF p.3]

variant_label: Foreign exchange — Oil share of Iran's foreign exchange earnings
notes: Oil revenues constitute over 90 percent of Iran's foreign exchange earnings. [cite: CIA Directorate of Intelligence, NESA M 86-20082, "Iran-Iraq: The Economic Balance", 6 June 1986, PDF p.3]

variant_label: Trade — Oil-funded imports share of Iran's food requirements
notes: Oil-funded imports provide 20-25 percent of Iran's food requirements, plus the vast bulk of its war materiel and essential industrial/transport/construction supplies. [cite: CIA Directorate of Intelligence, NESA M 86-20082, "Iran-Iraq: The Economic Balance", 6 June 1986, PDF p.3]

variant_label: Foreign exchange — Iran estimated 1986 foreign exchange revenues
notes: Estimated Iranian foreign exchange revenue for 1986: $8.5 billion; maintaining recent food/military import levels would use almost three-fourths of this. [cite: CIA Directorate of Intelligence, NESA M 86-20082, "Iran-Iraq: The Economic Balance", 6 June 1986, PDF p.3]

variant_label: Trade — Iran required import cut
notes: Tehran will need to slash imports by one-third in 1986 despite expected foreign loans from Japan and West European trading partners. [cite: CIA Directorate of Intelligence, NESA M 86-20082, "Iran-Iraq: The Economic Balance", 6 June 1986, PDF p.3]

variant_label: Trade — Iran cumulative import decline since 1984
notes: A one-third 1986 cut would represent about a 50 percent decline in Iran's imports since 1984. [cite: CIA Directorate of Intelligence, NESA M 86-20082, "Iran-Iraq: The Economic Balance", 6 June 1986, PDF p.3]

variant_label: Foreign exchange — Iran foreign exchange reserve cushion
notes: Iran's limited foreign exchange assets amount to about three months of imports, serving as a cushion against a complete oil-export cutoff -- probably will avoid drawing these down. [cite: CIA Directorate of Intelligence, NESA M 86-20082, "Iran-Iraq: The Economic Balance", 6 June 1986, PDF p.3]

variant_label: Labor & Employment — Iran official unemployment rate
notes: Official Iranian estimates place unemployment at 15 percent, or about 2 million people. [cite: CIA Directorate of Intelligence, NESA M 86-20082, "Iran-Iraq: The Economic Balance", 6 June 1986, PDF p.4]

variant_label: Labor & Employment — Iran official unemployment count
notes: Official estimate of approximately 2 million unemployed. [cite: CIA Directorate of Intelligence, NESA M 86-20082, "Iran-Iraq: The Economic Balance", 6 June 1986, PDF p.4]

variant_label: Labor & Employment — Iran 'false employment' + unemployment share of labor force
notes: Former head of Iran's Planning and Budget Ministry complained in April 1986 that "false employment" (government make-work) plus unemployment account for 39 percent of Iran's 12.3 million labor force, per press reports. [cite: CIA Directorate of Intelligence, NESA M 86-20082, "Iran-Iraq: The Economic Balance", 6 June 1986, PDF p.4]

variant_label: Labor & Employment — Iran total labor force
notes: Iran's labor force cited at 12.3 million as of this 1986 assessment. [cite: CIA Directorate of Intelligence, NESA M 86-20082, "Iran-Iraq: The Economic Balance", 6 June 1986, PDF p.4]

variant_label: Foreign exchange — Iraq estimated 1986 foreign exchange earnings
notes: Iraqi foreign exchange earnings estimated to fall to $7.4 billion in 1986. [cite: CIA Directorate of Intelligence, NESA M 86-20082, "Iran-Iraq: The Economic Balance", 6 June 1986, PDF p.4]

variant_label: Foreign exchange — Iraq 1985 foreign exchange earnings
notes: Iraqi foreign exchange earnings in 1985 were $11.7 billion, the prior-year baseline for the 1986 decline. [cite: CIA Directorate of Intelligence, NESA M 86-20082, "Iran-Iraq: The Economic Balance", 6 June 1986, PDF p.4]

variant_label: Trade — Iraq required import cut
notes: Baghdad will need to cut imports by 30-35 percent given the FX earnings decline; deeper if Saudi Arabia/Kuwait do not provide additional cash assistance. [cite: CIA Directorate of Intelligence, NESA M 86-20082, "Iran-Iraq: The Economic Balance", 6 June 1986, PDF p.4]

variant_label: Foreign exchange — Oil exports share of Iraq's foreign exchange earnings
notes: Iraqi oil exports, already near capacity, represent 98 percent of foreign exchange earnings -- Baghdad cannot appreciably raise revenues. [cite: CIA Directorate of Intelligence, NESA M 86-20082, "Iran-Iraq: The Economic Balance", 6 June 1986, PDF p.4]

variant_label: External debt — Iraq missed payment to France (first-time default)
notes: US Embassy in Baghdad reports Iraq failed for the first time to make a $120 million payment to France, its major creditor and an important arms supplier; Paris suspended short/medium-term credit as a result. [cite: CIA Directorate of Intelligence, NESA M 86-20082, "Iran-Iraq: The Economic Balance", 6 June 1986, PDF p.5]

variant_label: External debt — Iraq letters-of-credit payment failures
notes: Baghdad's failure to honor up to $1 billion in letters of credit -- the principal manner of conducting trade -- caused many banks to refuse handling Iraqi L/Cs. [cite: CIA Directorate of Intelligence, NESA M 86-20082, "Iran-Iraq: The Economic Balance", 6 June 1986, PDF p.5]

variant_label: External debt — Iraq debt payments sought for rescheduling
notes: Iraq reportedly seeking to reschedule at least $3 billion in debt payments due in 1986. [cite: CIA Directorate of Intelligence, NESA M 86-20082, "Iran-Iraq: The Economic Balance", 6 June 1986, PDF p.5]

variant_label: Foreign exchange — Iraq required FX share to maintain military/food imports
notes: To maintain recent import levels of military goods and food, Iraq would have to use almost 90 percent of its estimated 1986 foreign exchange revenues. [cite: CIA Directorate of Intelligence, NESA M 86-20082, "Iran-Iraq: The Economic Balance", 6 June 1986, PDF p.5]

variant_label: Labor & Employment — Iraq foreign workers
notes: Iraq had about 1.2 million foreign workers in 1985; the regime is trying to reduce this number despite a war-intensified labor shortage. [cite: CIA Directorate of Intelligence, NESA M 86-20082, "Iran-Iraq: The Economic Balance", 6 June 1986, PDF p.5]

variant_label: Foreign exchange — Tehran readily-accessible foreign exchange assets
notes: Tehran has about $3 billion in readily accessible foreign exchange assets, versus almost none for Baghdad. [cite: CIA Directorate of Intelligence, NESA M 86-20082, "Iran-Iraq: The Economic Balance", 6 June 1986, PDF p.7]

variant_label: External debt — Iraq debt owed to non-Arab creditors
notes: Iraq owes $9-10 billion to non-Arab debtors and has virtually no access to further credit, versus Iran's negligible foreign debt. [cite: CIA Directorate of Intelligence, NESA M 86-20082, "Iran-Iraq: The Economic Balance", 6 June 1986, PDF p.7]

variant_label: Oil production & trade — New Iraq pipeline through Turkey completion
notes: A new Iraqi oil pipeline through Turkey is scheduled for completion in May 1987, expected to raise export capacity. [cite: CIA Directorate of Intelligence, NESA M 86-20082, "Iran-Iraq: The Economic Balance", 6 June 1986, PDF p.7]

variant_label: Macro / National Accounts — Oil sector share of domestic output, Iraq
notes: Two-thirds of Iraq's domestic output is directly linked to oil. [cite: CIA Directorate of Intelligence, NESA M 86-20082, "Iran-Iraq: The Economic Balance", 6 June 1986, PDF p.7]

variant_label: Macro / National Accounts — Oil sector share of domestic output, Iran
notes: The oil sector contributes only about one-third of domestic output in Iran, versus two-thirds for Iraq. [cite: CIA Directorate of Intelligence, NESA M 86-20082, "Iran-Iraq: The Economic Balance", 6 June 1986, PDF p.7]

variant_label: Foreign aid — Saudi/Kuwait financial support to Iraq since war start, cumulative
notes: About $25 billion in financial support from Saudi Arabia and Kuwait since the beginning of the war has helped maintain Iraqi living standards. [cite: CIA Directorate of Intelligence, NESA M 86-20082, "Iran-Iraq: The Economic Balance", 6 June 1986, PDF p.7]

variant_label: Oil production & trade — Decline in Iraq oil revenues this year
notes: Total Arab aid this year is unlikely to offset the $4.3 billion decline in Iraqi oil revenues unless substantial additional cash payments are made. [cite: CIA Directorate of Intelligence, NESA M 86-20082, "Iran-Iraq: The Economic Balance", 6 June 1986, PDF p.7]

variant_label: Foreign aid — Saudi/Kuwait financial assistance to Iraq, 1985 level
notes: Financial assistance from Saudi Arabia and Kuwait for 1986 will probably be even less than last year's (1985) $2.0-2.5 billion, absent substantial additional cash payments. [cite: CIA Directorate of Intelligence, NESA M 86-20082, "Iran-Iraq: The Economic Balance", 6 June 1986, PDF p.7]

variant_label: Foreign aid — Iraq oil aid figure announced by Baghdad
notes: The 300,000 b/d oil-aid figure announced by Baghdad (crude sold on Iraq's behalf, mainly via the Neutral Zone) may be overstated because of low Neutral Zone production. [cite: CIA Directorate of Intelligence, NESA M 86-20082, "Iran-Iraq: The Economic Balance", 6 June 1986, PDF p.7]

variant_label: Oil production & trade — Neutral Zone share of Iraq's oil aid
notes: Low production in the Neutral Zone, which accounts for 80 percent of Iraq's oil aid, may make the 300,000 b/d aid figure overstated. [cite: CIA Directorate of Intelligence, NESA M 86-20082, "Iran-Iraq: The Economic Balance", 6 June 1986, PDF p.7]

variant_label: Oil production & trade — Iraq-Saudi pipeline flow restriction
notes: Saudi Arabia has restricted Iraqi crude flow through the Iraq-Saudi pipeline to about 150,000 b/d below its 500,000 b/d capacity, depriving Baghdad of several hundred million dollars since last November (1985). [cite: CIA Directorate of Intelligence, NESA M 86-20082, "Iran-Iraq: The Economic Balance", 6 June 1986, PDF p.7]

variant_label: Oil production & trade — Iraq-Saudi pipeline capacity
notes: Full nameplate capacity of the Iraq-Saudi pipeline, against which the ~150,000 b/d restriction is measured. [cite: CIA Directorate of Intelligence, NESA M 86-20082, "Iran-Iraq: The Economic Balance", 6 June 1986, PDF p.7]

variant_label: Conflict & Violence — Iranian tanker attacks on Saudi shipping
notes: In early May 1986 Iran attacked three Saudi tankers in the Gulf to underscore its warning that Saudi Arabia/Kuwait should restrain oil production and discontinue economic support for Iraq. [cite: CIA Directorate of Intelligence, NESA M 86-20082, "Iran-Iraq: The Economic Balance", 6 June 1986, PDF p.6]
